top_signals.title: Trump says U.S. will 'go right back to dropping bombs' if he doesn't like Iran deal
top_signals.summary: U.S. President Donald Trump said Wednesday at the G7 conference that the U.S. will "go right back to dropping bombs" if he doesn't like the Iran deal.
top_signals.why_it_matters: Geopolitical escalation affecting commodities, shipping and cross-asset volatility.

top_signals.title: G7 Aims to Ensure China Supplies No More Than 60% of Rare Earths
top_signals.summary: The Group of Seven countries have agreed that no single country should supply more than 60% of their imports of critical minerals by 2030 in a effort to reduce their reliance on China, according to people familiar with the discussions.
top_signals.why_it_matters: Geopolitical escalation affecting commodities, shipping and cross-asset volatility.

top_signals.title: Starmer Hot Mic Moment Suggests India Trade Row Breakthrough
top_signals.summary: UK Prime Minister Keir Starmer raised expectations that Britain has resolved a trade impasse with India, when he and Indian Prime Minister Narendra Modi were caught on a microphone at the G7 summit in France saying some form of agreement had been reached.
top_signals.why_it_matters: AI and semiconductor developments impacting technology leadership and capex cycles.
